description_long: This object is a cylindrical white ceramic jar with a slightly domed lid, sealed with a thick, brownish material around the rim, possibly wax or resin. The jar features black printed text in French and Spanish, indicating it is an extract of meat produced by the company Giebert & Cie, associated with the Liebig brand and originating from Fray-Bentos, Uruguay. The surface of the jar is smooth and glossy, with visible cracks and signs of age, suggesting it is an antique or vintage medicine container. The lid appears to be integrated with the jar, and a red emblem or seal is present at the center of the top, possibly serving as a brand mark or tamper-evident feature. The overall appearance is utilitarian yet historic, with the text and emblem providing both identification and authenticity. The jar is likely used to store a medicinal or nutritional extract, and its robust construction would protect the contents from contamination.
description: A vintage white ceramic jar with black printed text, sealed with a brownish material and topped with a red emblem, used for storing medicinal meat extract.
description_view_0: Front view shows the main label with detailed black text, visible cracks, and the brown seal around the lid.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the continuation of the label text and the thick, uneven brown seal at the top.
description_view_2: The rear view displays more of the label, focusing on the words 'Liebig' and 'Viande', with fewer visible cracks.
description_view_3: This angled view emphasizes the jar's cylindrical shape, the brown sealing material, and partial label text.
attribute_label_text: EXTRACTUM CARNIS LIEBIG DE LA SOCIÉTÉ DE Fray-Bentos URUGUAY GIEBERT & CIE AGENT GENERAL JOS BENNERT ANVERS EXTRAIT DE VIANDE préparé D'APRÈS LA MÉTHODE DU PROFESSEUR LIEBIG
attribute_brand_logo: red emblem or seal at the center of the lid
